_meta.description: Comprehensive JD role pattern definitions for resume feedback — SOTA production config

implicitExpectations: Ability to learn quickly from senior engineers
Basic understanding of software development lifecycle
Comfortable asking for help and clarifying requirements
Writes clean, readable, and testable code
Familiar with debugging tools
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD expects too much for a junior role — may have unrealistic expectations
resumeTips: Highlight academic projects, internships, or personal projects
Show Git commit history or open-source contributions
Include specific technologies used in coursework or side projects
Quantify project impact even at small scale

implicitExpectations: Independently delivers features end-to-end
Participates meaningfully in code reviews
Estimates tasks with reasonable accuracy
Understands database indexing and query optimization basics
Can debug production issues independently
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses culture red flags — approach with caution
resumeTips: Quantify impact: throughput, latency improvements, cost savings
Highlight ownership of specific services or modules
Show breadth across the stack if relevant
Include specific tech stack versions and tools

implicitExpectations: System design ownership and architectural decisions
Code review leadership with actionable feedback
Mentoring junior and mid-level engineers
Drives technical roadmap for a service or domain
Proactively identifies and reduces technical debt
Participates in on-call and incident response
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses culture red flags — approach with caution
resumeTips: Demonstrate leadership: decisions made, systems designed
Show scale: RPS, data volume, team size impacted
Highlight cross-team collaboration and influence
Include post-mortems or reliability improvements

implicitExpectations: Defines technical direction across multiple teams or entire engineering org
Resolves ambiguous, high-stakes architectural challenges
Partners with Product, Data, and Leadership stakeholders
Drives adoption of platform capabilities org-wide
Represents engineering perspective in strategic planning
Mentors senior engineers toward staff-level thinking
feedback.redFlagMessage: Staff roles requiring purely hands-on execution without org-level scope are mislabeled
resumeTips: Articulate org-level impact, not just team-level
Include examples of technical vision and persuasion
Show cross-functional influence and stakeholder alignment
Demonstrate navigating ambiguity and competing priorities

implicitExpectations: Implements pixel-perfect UI from design specs
Collaborates closely with designers and product managers
Understands browser rendering and performance implications
Writes accessible, semantic HTML
Cross-browser compatibility awareness
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD may conflate frontend with full-stack — clarify scope before applying
resumeTips: Link to portfolio, deployed apps, or GitHub projects
Highlight Core Web Vitals or Lighthouse scores improved
Show design collaboration and Figma handoff experience
Include accessibility or internationalization work

implicitExpectations: Architects frontend systems and design systems
Sets frontend best practices and code quality standards
Mentors junior and mid frontend engineers
Deep understanding of browser internals and rendering pipeline
Drives performance culture across the frontend team
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses culture red flags — approach with caution
resumeTips: Quantify Core Web Vitals improvements (LCP, CLS, FID)
Highlight design system ownership and adoption
Show cross-browser and cross-device expertise
Include documentation and RFC authoring

implicitExpectations: Comfortable switching between frontend and backend tasks
Can own a feature from database schema to UI implementation
Understands trade-offs between different layers of the stack
Flexible and adaptable to shifting priorities
feedback.redFlagMessage: Full-stack roles can be a red flag for underfunded teams — assess team size and specialization carefully
resumeTips: Show examples of features you owned end-to-end
Clarify depth vs breadth of each skill
Include tech stack clearly in each work experience
Highlight shipped products, not just tasks completed

implicitExpectations: Owns technical direction for product features across the full stack
Collaborates deeply with product and design
Sets coding standards and runs code reviews
Identifies architectural bottlenecks proactively
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD may expect staff-level impact without appropriate compensation — negotiate carefully
resumeTips: Highlight products shipped with measurable user or business impact
Show ownership of system architecture decisions
Demonstrate depth in at least one layer of the stack
Include mentorship and team collaboration examples

implicitExpectations: Can take ML models from notebook to production
Understands trade-offs between model accuracy and latency
Writes production-quality Python for model serving
Collaborates with data engineers and backend engineers
Familiar with model monitoring and drift detection
feedback.redFlagMessage: ML role may be overly academic — assess whether engineering or research is the primary expectation
resumeTips: Show models deployed to production with measurable impact
Include dataset sizes, model metrics (precision, recall, AUC)
Highlight latency and throughput optimizations
Show collaboration with product teams on ML features

implicitExpectations: Designs and owns end-to-end ML systems
Sets ML platform standards and best practices
Mentors ML engineers and data scientists
Drives ML roadmap and prioritization
Deep understanding of model serving and inference optimization
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses culture red flags or gatekeeping credentials — approach with caution
resumeTips: Quantify model improvements (accuracy gains, latency reduction)
Show ML system design at scale (billions of predictions, etc.)
Include any publications, patents, or open-source contributions
Demonstrate cross-functional ML impact (product, revenue, safety)

implicitExpectations: Translates business problems into analytical questions
Communicates findings clearly to non-technical stakeholders
Designs rigorous experiments and interprets results
Collaborates with engineers on data pipeline requirements
Documents analyses and maintains reproducibility
feedback.redFlagMessage: Data science roles expecting production ML without engineering support may be overly broad
resumeTips: Show business impact of analyses (revenue, retention, cost)
Highlight statistical rigor and experimental design
Include stakeholder communication and presentation examples
Show data storytelling with visualizations

implicitExpectations: Drives data science strategy for a product area
Mentors junior data scientists
Partners with engineering on scalable ML deployments
Influences product strategy with data insights
Independently scopes and executes complex analytical projects
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses red flags or credential gatekeeping — assess team structure carefully
resumeTips: Quantify analytical impact on key product metrics
Show complex experiment design and causal inference
Demonstrate leadership in defining metrics and KPIs
Include cross-functional influence and stakeholder management

implicitExpectations: Builds and maintains robust data pipelines
Ensures data quality and observability
Collaborates with data scientists and analysts on requirements
Understands data modeling and schema design
Manages data infrastructure and cost optimization
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses culture red flags — approach with caution
resumeTips: Quantify data volumes processed (TBs, PBs, records/day)
Highlight data quality improvements and SLA achievements
Show infrastructure cost optimizations
Include data modeling and warehousing design decisions

implicitExpectations: Architects the data platform and data infrastructure strategy
Establishes data engineering best practices and standards
Mentors junior and mid data engineers
Drives data quality and observability culture
Partners with leadership on data strategy
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses culture red flags — approach with caution
resumeTips: Show data platform architecture decisions and their impact
Quantify reliability improvements (SLA, data freshness)
Highlight cross-team data enablement work
Include cost optimization and scalability achievements

implicitExpectations: Maintains and improves CI/CD pipelines
Manages cloud infrastructure as code
On-call rotation for infrastructure incidents
Collaborates with engineering teams on deployment practices
Drives infrastructure cost awareness and optimization
feedback.redFlagMessage: Excessive on-call expectations without rotation or compensation should be scrutinized
resumeTips: Quantify uptime and SLA achievements
Show infrastructure cost savings percentages
Highlight deployment frequency and change failure rate improvements
Include incident response and postmortem experience

implicitExpectations: Defines SLO framework and reliability culture
Architects platform engineering capabilities
Mentors junior SREs and DevOps engineers
Drives incident response process improvements
Partners with engineering leadership on platform strategy
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses culture red flags — assess on-call expectations carefully
resumeTips: Quantify reliability improvements (MTTR, availability %)
Show platform adoption and developer experience improvements
Include SLO design and error budget management
Highlight cost optimization and FinOps work

implicitExpectations: Reviews code and architecture for security vulnerabilities
Collaborates with engineering teams on secure development practices
Manages vulnerability disclosure and remediation
Conducts security assessments and threat modeling
Maintains security tooling and automation
feedback.redFlagMessage: Security roles with no peer review culture can be a red flag — assess team structure
resumeTips: Highlight specific vulnerabilities found and remediated
Show security program improvements (coverage, MTTR)
Include compliance and audit experience
Demonstrate cross-team security enablement work

implicitExpectations: Defines security architecture and standards for the organization
Leads security reviews for major platform changes
Builds and maintains security champions program
Partners with compliance and legal on regulatory requirements
Mentors security engineers and enables engineering teams
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses culture red flags — assess team structure and organizational security maturity
resumeTips: Quantify security posture improvements (vulnerabilities reduced, coverage)
Show cross-organizational security program leadership
Include compliance certification achievements
Demonstrate security culture change management

implicitExpectations: Delivers features following Apple HIG guidelines
Manages App Store submission and review process
Handles crashes and performance profiling with Instruments
Collaborates with backend engineers on API contracts
Maintains backward compatibility with supported iOS versions
feedback.redFlagMessage: iOS-plus-Android role may indicate underfunded mobile team — verify scope
resumeTips: Link to published App Store applications
Highlight download counts, ratings, or active users
Show performance optimizations (startup time, memory)
Include App Store review navigation experience

implicitExpectations: Architects iOS app modules and shared frameworks
Sets coding standards and leads iOS code reviews
Mentors junior iOS developers
Drives performance and reliability culture for mobile
Manages complex App Store submission processes
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses culture red flags — approach with caution
resumeTips: Show app architecture decisions and their impact
Quantify performance improvements and crash rate reductions
Highlight modularization and maintainability work
Include App Store Connect metrics where possible

implicitExpectations: Delivers Android features following Material Design guidelines
Manages Google Play Store submission process
Handles device fragmentation and backward compatibility
Profiles and optimizes app performance with Android Profiler
Collaborates with backend on API design
feedback.redFlagMessage: Android-plus-iOS role may indicate underfunded mobile team — verify scope
resumeTips: Link to published Play Store applications
Highlight install counts, ratings, or active users
Show ANR and crash rate improvements
Include Jetpack Compose migration experience if applicable

implicitExpectations: Architects Android app modules and shared libraries
Sets coding standards and leads Android code reviews
Mentors junior Android developers
Drives performance and reliability culture for Android
Manages complex Play Store deployment pipelines
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses culture red flags — approach with caution
resumeTips: Show app architecture decisions and measurable impact
Quantify crash rate and ANR rate improvements
Highlight modularization and maintainability contributions
Include Play Store metrics where possible

implicitExpectations: Owns the product backlog and sprint planning
Balances user needs with business objectives
Communicates product vision to engineering and design teams
Defines and tracks product KPIs
Manages stakeholder expectations across functions
feedback.redFlagMessage: PM role with undefined scope or unrealistic ownership may indicate organizational dysfunction
resumeTips: Quantify product impact: MAU growth, revenue, retention
Show discovery-to-delivery process ownership
Include cross-functional collaboration examples
Highlight data-driven decision making

implicitExpectations: Owns a product area with full P&L awareness
Manages or mentors junior PMs
Partners with engineering leadership on technical strategy
Represents product perspective in executive meetings
Sets quarterly OKRs and tracks team performance
feedback.redFlagMessage: Vague senior PM JDs without defined scope or team context may reflect organizational immaturity
resumeTips: Show strategic product bets and their outcomes
Include revenue or growth impact tied to your roadmap
Demonstrate stakeholder management at VP/C-level
Highlight PM mentorship or team building

implicitExpectations: Creates wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs
Facilitates user research and synthesizes findings
Collaborates with product managers on feature scope
Ensures design system consistency
Presents design rationale to stakeholders
feedback.redFlagMessage: Roles that skip research in favor of pure visual output may undervalue UX function
resumeTips: Include portfolio URL prominently
Show complete design process, not just final output
Quantify usability improvements (task completion, error rates)
Highlight cross-functional collaboration stories

implicitExpectations: Defines design direction and sets quality bar for the team
Mentors junior and mid designers
Partners with engineering on design system implementation
Advocates for user experience in product and business decisions
Runs complex research programs and synthesizes insights
feedback.redFlagMessage: Senior UX roles without dedicated research time or design system ownership may be misleveled
resumeTips: Show design system contributions and adoption
Include complex research programs and their business impact
Demonstrate stakeholder influence and executive communication
Highlight mentorship and design team culture building

implicitExpectations: Manages a team of 4–10 engineers
Responsible for team delivery, quality, and morale
Partners with product and design on roadmap
Conducts performance reviews and develops talent
Recruits and grows the engineering team
feedback.redFlagMessage: Player-coach roles at scale are a red flag — clarify engineering vs management time expectations
resumeTips: Quantify team size and growth under your leadership
Show engineering outcomes: delivery velocity, quality improvements
Highlight retention and team health metrics
Include hiring volume and diversity initiatives

implicitExpectations: Manages multiple engineering managers and their teams
Sets engineering culture and organizational values
Represents engineering in executive leadership discussions
Owns engineering budget and headcount decisions
Drives org-level engineering strategy
feedback.redFlagMessage: Director roles expecting IC-level coding output may reflect organizational misunderstanding of leadership functions
resumeTips: Show org-level impact: team size, departments led
Quantify engineering org health improvements
Demonstrate business impact of engineering investments
Include board or executive communication examples

implicitExpectations: Writes and maintains test cases and test plans
Participates in sprint ceremonies and story review
Identifies edge cases and regression risks
Collaborates closely with developers on bug reproduction
Maintains test automation suite
feedback.redFlagMessage: Undefined QA process or over-reliance on manual testing at scale may indicate technical debt
resumeTips: Quantify test coverage improvements
Show bug detection rates and regression reduction
Highlight automation framework contributions
Include release quality metrics

implicitExpectations: Defines quality strategy and test architecture
Mentors junior QA engineers
Drives shift-left testing culture across engineering
Establishes quality metrics and OKRs
Partners with DevOps on test pipeline integration
feedback.redFlagMessage: JD uses culture red flags — approach with caution
resumeTips: Show quality metric improvements (defect escape rate, coverage)
Highlight test automation framework architecture
Include shift-left testing adoption and impact
Demonstrate cross-team quality enablement

implicitExpectations: Bridges communication between business and technology teams
Documents detailed requirements and acceptance criteria
Facilitates workshops and requirements sessions
Validates that delivered solutions meet business needs
Maintains process documentation and data dictionaries
feedback.redFlagMessage: BA + PM combined role may indicate insufficient resourcing — clarify scope
resumeTips: Show requirements delivered that led to successful project outcomes
Include stakeholder communication and facilitation examples
Quantify business process improvements
Highlight cross-functional collaboration

implicitExpectations: Leads complex, enterprise-level requirements projects
Mentors junior business analysts
Partners with C-level stakeholders on strategic initiatives
Drives business process transformation programs
Acts as the bridge between technology investment and business value
feedback.redFlagMessage: Senior BA roles with undefined scope or mixed PM responsibilities may indicate organizational dysfunction
resumeTips: Show strategic projects and their business value delivered
Include executive stakeholder management experience
Quantify process improvements and cost savings
Highlight cross-departmental program leadership
